title: 'SWIM' Tops Billboard Global 200 for Fourth Consecutive Week; ARIRANG Holds #3 on Billboard 200
detail: Billboard confirmed on April 22, 2026 that 'SWIM' topped the Billboard Global 200 for a fourth consecutive week — extending BTS's record-setting streak on the global chart. Concurrently, ARIRANG held #3 on the Billboard 200 (chart dated April 25, ~78,000 equivalent album units) in its fourth tracking week — the best fourth-week chart position by any K-pop album in US chart history. Additional ARIRANG tracks charting on the Hot 100 include 'Body to Body' (#69), '2.0' (#88), and 'Hooligan' (#90). SWIM also held #10 on the Hot 100. Korea Herald and Starnews Korea reported the chart milestones as BTS enters the final pre-tour push ahead of the April 25 North America leg opener in Tampa.

media.caption: BTS tops Billboard Global charts for fourth consecutive week as ARIRANG holds strong in week 4

title: Tampa Closes Boulevard for BTS Merchandise Tent — North America Tour Opener in Three Days
detail: The City of Tampa issued a traffic advisory on April 22, 2026: Tampa Bay Boulevard in front of Raymond James Stadium was closed starting April 22 at 6:00 AM for setup of a large-scale BTS merchandise tent ahead of three sold-out ARIRANG World Tour shows (April 25, 26, 28). Fan merchandise sales were set to begin April 23, drawing ARMY from across North America days before the concerts. The three-night Tampa run marks BTS's first US concerts since their 2021 SoFi Stadium run and the launch of the North American leg of the 82-show ARIRANG World Tour. Each night is expected to draw approximately 65,000 fans at Raymond James Stadium, totaling ~195,000 attendees across the Tampa dates.

media.caption: Tampa shuts down roadway for BTS merchandise sales ahead of three sold-out ARIRANG World Tour stadium shows
